A column reference is 'table.column' with exactly one dot, so rsplit('.', 1) and rsplit('.', 2) return the same two parts.

The postgresql type map is identity (uuid->uuid, etc.), so emptying or swapping a postgres KEY makes _sql_type fall to its default, which returns the abstract type unchanged — the same string the identity map would have produced. Verified across all types and dialects.

Emptying/swapping the 'postgresql' key in _RECONSTRUCTION_OPS makes requires_reconstruction fall to the default empty frozenset, which is exactly what the postgres entry is — no operation requires reconstruction on postgres either way. Verified.

Removing the trailing `return None` at the end of _reconstruct's success path lets the function fall off its end and return None implicitly — identical.
honest-persist/src/honest_persist/schema: A `return None` reached on the success path; removing it lets the function fall off its end and return None implicitly — identical. Verified across schema-diff inputs.
TypedDict total= is a static-only annotation with no runtime effect; total=True and total=False produce an identical class whose instances are plain dicts, and the schema dicts are assembled explicitly, so no constructible input distinguishes them.

honest-persist/src/honest_persist/cutove: A foreign-key reference is 'table.column' with exactly one dot, so rsplit('.', 1)[0] and rsplit('.', 2)[0] return the same table part.
honest-persist/src/honest_persist/loader: _unwrap_optional runs only on an Optional annotation (X | None), whose non-None members are exactly one, so [0] and [-1] are the same element. Verified across Optional fields.

The _default is only consumed via default_sql, which returns None for both PydanticUndefined and None; the and->or only changes whether _default is stored as one of those, never the rendered column default. Verified the loaded columns are identical.

_is_optional's guard returns False for a non-generic annotation; removing it falls through to `type(None) in get_args(annotation)`, and get_args of a non-generic is (), so the result is False either way. Verified.
As above: the guard's `return False` -> pass falls through to `type(None) in get_args(annotation)`, which is False for a non-generic (empty get_args). Verified.

Swapping the `str` key in _PY_TO_SQL drops str from the map, but _sql_type falls back to `.get(name, "text")` whose default is exactly "text" — str's mapping — so str still resolves to text. Verified.
honest-persist/src/honest_persist/django: A text-mapping entry: CharField/TextField/SlugField map to 'text' which is also the _dj_type fallback default, and EmailField reports get_internal_type()=='CharField' (a dead map entry), so emptying or swapping these keys/values leaves every field still resolving to 'text'. Verified the loaded columns are unchanged.

honest-persist/src/honest_persist/abstra: Emptying the 'postgresql' key makes _SEED_VERB.get('postgresql') miss and hit the default, which is exactly 'INSERT' — postgresql's value — so the verb is unchanged. Verified.
Seed rows are always single-column (value), so the ', ' separator in the placeholder join is never used; emptying it cannot change any single-column seed insert. Verified across the seed battery.
The emptied string is the ', ' separator joining the column-name list, which is single-element for every seed row, so it is never used. Verified across the seed battery.
honest-persist/src/honest_persist/ambigu: Confidence is only ever 0.0, 0.7, or 1.0 — never exactly 0.5 — so `>= 0.5` and `> 0.5` partition the values identically (0.7 and 1.0 pass, 0.0 fails) for every reachable input.
honest-persist/src/honest_persist/pool.p: The mutated 1 is the maxsplit of key.split(':', 1); a pool key is 'database:credential' with exactly one colon, so split(':', 1) and split(':', 2) return the same parts and [0] is the database either way.
The number scan's start offset: the digit loop converges to the same closing position and the value is sliced from index (always including the '-'), so +1 vs +2 yields the same number. Battery-verified equivalent.
The advance after an unknown token; parse_check rejects any unknown token by type regardless of where index next lands, so skipping an extra char cannot change the fault. Battery-verified.
A one-clause junction wrapped in an AND/OR node instead of returned bare: check_holds(all([c]))==check_holds(c) and any([c])==c, and no test asserts the tree shape. Battery-verified.
As above: removing the single-clause shortcut wraps one clause in an AND/OR node, eval-equivalent. Battery-verified.
As above: the single-clause return becomes a wrap, eval-equivalent. Battery-verified.
The unknown token's value key; parse_check detects unknown tokens by type only and the fault message uses the whole expression, so the value is never read. Battery-verified.
Removing the value-list opening-paren guard lets the parser advance one token and then fail to find a literal term, returning None — the same 'cannot be compiled' fault. Battery-verified.
An error-path None return that falls through to implicit None — identical. Battery-verified across the malformed inputs.
As above: a parser error-path None return falling through to an equivalent None. Battery-verified.
As above: an error-path None guard whose pass falls through to an equivalent None. Battery-verified.
honest-persist/src/honest_persist/migrat: NOT equivalent — the bare injected I/O seam of _inspect_postgresql (two catalog reads and a return): all decidable logic is pure (_pg_tables, _pg_column, _pg_indexes, _owned_tables, _read_object_registry's parse, the query builders) and mutation-tested; reading a response key wrong, swapping ok for err, or dropping a read manifests only against a live connection. Caught by the real-PostgreSQL probe (postgres_fixture) in every coverage and normal suite run, excluded from the per-mutant loop because PostgreSQL has no in-memory mode and a real server per mutant is prohibitively slow (mirrors how _inspect_sqlite's identical seam is caught by cheap in-memory SQLite).
